steps.title: Put both equations in graphable form
steps.content: Rewrite the system as $$y=9x+13$$ and $$y=\frac{5}{2}x.$$ This makes it easy to graph or substitute.

steps.title: Solve the intersection
steps.content: Set the right sides equal: $$9x+13=\frac{5}{2}x.$$ Multiply by $2$: $18x+26=5x$, so $-13x=26$ and $x=-2$. Then $y=\frac{5}{2}(-2)=-5$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The solution is $(-2,-5)$. Reversing the ordered pair or using other coordinates does not satisfy both equations, so C is correct.
